title: [FIRRTL] width inference (mux) mismatch
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input _tmp15: UInt<22>
    output tmp19: UInt
    tmp19 <= mux(UInt<1>(1), UInt(192), _tmp15)
```

Compiled with `firtool` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [21:0] _tmp15,
  output [7:0]  tmp19);

  assign tmp19 = 8'hC0;	// a_top_mod.fir:2:3, :5:30
endmodule
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [21:0] _tmp15,
  output [21:0] tmp19
);
  assign tmp19 = 22'hc0;
endmodule
```

tmp19 width is different

`Yosys 0.9+3911 (git sha1 4b31223e, clang 11.0.1 -fPIC -Os)` reports formal mismatch:

```
ERROR: Can't match gold port `tmp19_gold' to a gate port.
```
